novel_features.name: Saved-commute one-shot
novel_features.description: Run a saved commute in one keystroke; resolves origin, destination, and time-window from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the local saved_routes row with a live OpenLDBWS departure board call, including a destination filter and a time-window narrowing. No competing UK rail tool remembers your route.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the user's named recurring route resolved from local state, not a fresh A->B planning call.

novel_features.name: Offline station search
novel_features.description: FTS5 search over the local UK CRS code table; resolves Paddington -> PAD with no network call.
novel_features.rationale: Pure SQLite FTS5 over the shipped station list. No competing tool exposes an offline CRS resolver; agents currently hallucinate codes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this whenever the user names a station in prose; resolve to CRS once, then call any other uk-train-goat command.

novel_features.name: Saved-routes morning status
novel_features.description: Fans out across every saved route and prints a single ranked status table for all your commutes.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-entity local query: parallel calls to GetDepartureBoard for each saved row, merged into one view. No single OpenLDBWS endpoint returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this on disruption mornings when a user has multiple saved commutes and wants one answer, not N separate board calls.

novel_features.name: Service delay-reason briefing
novel_features.description: Combines GetServiceDetails delay/cancel reasons with adjacent operator alerts into one screen explaining what is going wrong with one service.
novel_features.rationale: Composes the service-detail payload with the surrounding NRCC operator-alert messages that the National Rail website buries behind clicks. Service-specific content pattern.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user wants to understand a delay, not just see it.

novel_features.name: Disruption-aware journey ranking
novel_features.description: Ranks A->B options by combined scheduled-time, current delay, and platform-known signal so on-time-but-later beats earlier-but-late.
novel_features.rationale: Chains journey + per-result GetServiceDetails so the ranking reflects current disruption, not the static timetable a thin wrapper would surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for trip planning during disruption; choosing the earliest scheduled departure is often wrong when delays exist.

novel_features.name: Recent-journey replay
novel_features.description: Re-runs your recent A->B searches with fresh live data, side-by-side, so iterative trip planning is one keystroke per refresh.
novel_features.rationale: Reads search_history rows and reissues each as a live journey query. Cross-entity local x live join; no API exposes this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user is iterating dates or origins for one trip; every previous query stays one keystroke away.

novel_features.name: Multi-origin board fan-out
novel_features.description: Accept a comma-separated list of CRS codes and merge departures across all of them into one ranked time-ordered list.
novel_features.rationale: Parallel GetDepartureBoard calls per CRS, deduplicated by service ID, ranked by absolute departure time. Cross-source merge no single OpenLDBWS call provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the next London-bound (or other regional cluster) departure across multiple terminals at once.

novel_features.name: Eval-grader 9th quality gate
novel_features.description: Programmatic eval grader that scores an LLM agent against a fixture suite of natural-language UK-rail prompts; 80% pass-rate threshold blocks ship.
novel_features.rationale: Pins MCP tool descriptions to behavioral assertions; failed evals surface tool-description ambiguity before users hit it. Agent-shaped output is the product.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this in CI on changes to internal/cli or internal/mcp; failing evals mean an agent will pick the wrong tool or wrong args.

narrative.headline: The only Go-native, agent-native, MCP-exposed UK rail CLI; live boards, journey planning, and a local SQLite store that remembers your commute.
narrative.value_prop: uk-train-goat wraps the free National Rail OpenLDBWS API with a Cobra command tree, an MCP server, and an offline station database. Live departures, arrivals, journey planning A->B, and service status all run from one terminal command and ship with a programmatic eval grader that pins tool descriptions for LLM agents.

quickstart.comment: Set your free OpenLDBWS access token; obtain one at realtime.nationalrail.co.uk/OpenLDBWSRegistration
Populate the local SQLite station list once; powers offline CRS lookup
Resolve a station name to its CRS code with no network call
Show the next 5 departures from Paddington
Plan a journey A->B and pipe to jq or an agent

troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `uk-train-goat-pp-cli auth login` with a token from realtime.nationalrail.co.uk/OpenLDBWSRegistration. The CLI uses LDBWS_API_TOKEN, not the wrapper's NR_ACCESS_TOKEN.
Run `uk-train-goat-pp-cli sync` to populate the local station table; first install ships an empty store.
fare scrapes nationalrail.co.uk and is marked experimental; if the upstream layout drifted, file an issue. Do not depend on fare data for booking.
Check connectivity to lite.realtime.nationalrail.co.uk; the API runs there over HTTPS.

narrative.when_to_use: Use uk-train-goat for any UK National Rail question an agent or terminal user has: live departures, live arrivals, journey planning between two stations on today or a future date, and service-status / delay-reason lookups. The local SQLite store gives every command offline CRS resolution and supports saved-commute features (`go`, `saved status`, `recent`) that a thin API wrapper cannot. Do not use uk-train-goat for booking, fare comparisons against Trainline, EU/Eurostar trains, or real-time push notifications; those are explicit non-goals for v0.1.

recipes.title: Daily morning commute check
recipes.explanation: Resolves the saved commute named 'morning' and returns the next departures filtered to your destination.

recipes.title: Compact multi-station agent query
recipes.explanation: Multi-origin fan-out + dotted-path field selection; tight payload for an LLM context window. --in / --within accept human-readable durations (30m, 1h).

recipes.title: Why is my train late
recipes.explanation: Composes service detail with NRCC operator alerts so the user sees the reason, not just the delay.

recipes.title: Iterative trip planning
recipes.explanation: Re-runs your recent journey queries with fresh live data; one keystroke per refresh.

recipes.title: CI eval gate
recipes.explanation: Runs the agent eval suite (set EVAL_AGENT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6 in your environment first; exits non-zero if pass rate drops below 80%).

trigger_phrases: is the X.YY to <station> on time
